title: centaurea transcaucasica subsp. georgica
intro: Centaurea transcaucasica subsp. georgica, commonly known as Caucasian cornflower, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to the Caucasus region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its various health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and soothing respiratory issues. The plant's flowers, leaves, and roots are used in herbal preparations, often in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, to leverage its medicinal properties. Furthermore, Centaurea transcaucasica subsp. georgica has been studied for its potential anti-cancer and antimicrobial properties, highlighting its importance in traditional medicine and modern research.
